title: Guidelines for Using a Salt:Chloroform Wash to Clean Up HMW DNA for PacBio
author: Miguel Flores
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bcuriwv6

steps.title: Bring the volume of HMW DNA...
steps.instruction: Bring the volume of HMW DNA up to 200 µL with Elution Buffer (EB) and label it as TUBE 1 .

steps.title: Add the following reagents to TUBE 1
steps.instruction: Add the following reagents to TUBE 1 : %s %s gDNA in EB

steps.title: Prepare Buffer A and set aside
steps.instruction: Prepare Buffer A and set aside: %s %s 5 M NaCl 500 µL of buffer will need to be prepared for each sample.

steps.title: Add 400 µL of Phenol:Chloroform:Isoamyl Alcohol...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL of Phenol:Chloroform:Isoamyl Alcohol (25:24:1), pH 8 to TUBE 1 .

steps.title: Invert the tube 20 times to mix
steps.instruction: Invert the tube 20 times to mix.

steps.title: Spin the tube at maximum speed...
steps.instruction: Spin the tube at maximum speed (at least 10 g) for 10 minutes at Room Temperature (RT).

steps.title: Carefully remove the aqueous layer, do not disturb the interface
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the aqueous layer, do not disturb the interface. Place into a clean 2 mL microcentrifuge tube. Label the tube as TUBE 2 .

steps.title: Add 400 µL of Buffer A...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL of Buffer A (from step 1) to TUBE 1 .

steps.title: Invert tube 20 times to mix
steps.instruction: Invert tube 20 times to mix.

steps.title: Spin tube at maximum speed (at...
steps.instruction: Spin tube at maximum speed (at least 10 g) for 10 minutes.

steps.title: Carefully remove the aqueous layer, do not disturb the interface
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the aqueous layer, do not disturb the interface. Place into TUBE 2 .

steps.title: . Measure the volume in TUBE 2
steps.instruction: . Measure the volume in TUBE 2 : __________ µL. It should be close to 800 µL.

steps.title: Add an equal volume of Chloroform:Isoamyl...
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume of Chloroform:Isoamyl Alcohol (24:1) to TUBE 2 .

steps.title: Invert tube 20 times to mix
steps.instruction: Invert tube 20 times to mix.

steps.title: Spin tube at maximum speed (at...
steps.instruction: Spin tube at maximum speed (at least 10g) for 10 minutes.

steps.title: Carefully remove the aqueous layer, do not disturb the interface
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the aqueous layer, do not disturb the interface. Place into a clean 2 mL microcentrifuge tube. Label the tube as TUBE 3 .

steps.title: Measure the volume in TUBE 3
steps.instruction: Measure the volume in TUBE 3 : __________ µL.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.3X volume of ethanol (99.99%) to TUBE 3 . This high-salt, low-ethanol mixture precipitates the excess polysaccharides while gDNA remains in the solution. _______ µl ( TUBE 3 ) x 0.3 = _________ µL of Ethanol

steps.title: Invert tube 20 times to mix
steps.instruction: Invert tube 20 times to mix.

steps.title: Spin tube at maximum speed (at...
steps.instruction: Spin tube at maximum speed (at least 10 g) for 15 minutes.

steps.title: Carefully remove the supernatant without disturbing the polysaccharide pellet
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the supernatant without disturbing the polysaccharide pellet. (Note that no visible pellet may be seen at this step). Place supernatant into a clean 2 mL microcentrifuge tube. Label the tube as TUBE 4 and measure the volume. TUBE 4 contains the gDNA.

. Add 1.7X volume of ethanol (99.99%) to TUBE 4 . The gDNA can be seen as falling out of the solution as long strands of gDNA; _______ µl ( TUBE 4 ) x 1.7 = _________ µL of Ethanol. If the final volume is larger than 2 mL, I recommend splitting the volume in two tubes (two tech reps).

steps.title: Invert tube 20 times to mix
steps.instruction: Invert tube 20 times to mix.

steps.title: Spin tube at maximum speed (at...
steps.instruction: Spin tube at maximum speed (at least 10 g) for 15 minutes. Discard supernatant with pipette, do not disturb the DNA pellet.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of 70% ethanol...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of 70% ethanol to DNA pellet to remove the excess salt; do not disturb the DNA pellet.

steps.title: Spin the tube at maximum speed...
steps.instruction: Spin the tube at maximum speed (at least 10 g) for 15 minutes. Discard supernatant with pipette, do not disturb the DNA pellet.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of 70% ethanol...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of 70% ethanol to DNA pellet to remove the excess salt; do not disturb the pellet.

steps.title: Spin the tube at maximum speed...
steps.instruction: Spin the tube at maximum speed (at least 10 g) for 15 minutes. Carefully remove the supernatant; do not disturb the DNA pellet.

steps.title: Quick spin to gather the residual...
steps.instruction: Quick spin to gather the residual ethanol at the bottom of the tube and carefully remove with a P20 tip.

steps.title: Let DNA pellet air dry for...
steps.instruction: Let DNA pellet air dry for 5 min at room temperature, taking care not to over dry.

steps.title: Resuspend the DNA pellet in 100...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the DNA pellet in 100 µL 10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8. Incubate at 4ºC with gently mixing overnight to resuspend. Store at 4ºC for use within one week, or store at -80ºC for long-term storage. If you have two technical reps, resuspend both DNA pellets with one round of 100 µL 10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.
